evals.prompt: When the user taps the info annotation in my MatrixScan AR app, print the barcode data. File: IntegratedBarcodeArWithInfoAnnotationViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate conformance, assigns annotation.delegate = self on the constructed BarcodeArInfoAnnotation inside the existing provider, implements the whole-annotation-tap callback, and prints barcode.data. Existing provider and integration code are preserved.

assertions.text: BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate conformance is present on the view controller
annotation.delegate = self is assigned inside the provider before returning / completing

annotation.isEntireAnnotationTappable = true is set (required for a tap anywhere on the info annotation to reach the whole-annotation delegate callback; default is false, which only fires per-element callbacks)
A BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate tap callback is implemented (e.g. the whole-annotation tap method documented on the BarcodeArInfoAnnotation page)
print( is used in the delegate callback
barcode.data is referenced in the delegate callback (directly or via optional binding)
BarcodeArViewUIDelegate is NOT used (that delegate is for highlight taps, not annotation taps)
barcodeArView.uiDelegate = self is NOT assigned (annotation interaction does not go through the view's uiDelegate)
Existing BarcodeArAnnotationProvider conformance is preserved
Existing annotation(for:completionHandler:) method is preserved
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeAr, barcodeArView, lifecycle, annotationProvider assignment) is preserved

evals.prompt: When the user taps the 'Details' button in the popover annotation, present a details screen (call showDetails(for: barcode.data ?? "")). When they tap 'Add to cart', call addToCart(barcode.data ?? ""). File: IntegratedBarcodeArViewController.swift. The popover should have these two buttons.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a BarcodeArAnnotationProvider that constructs a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation with two BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton instances, conforms to BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationDelegate, assigns the annotation's delegate property, and dispatches to showDetails / addToCart based on which button was tapped.

assertions.text: BarcodeArAnnotationProvider conformance is present
BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation is constructed with two BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton instances
'Details' appears as a button label
'Add to cart' appears as a button label
BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationDelegate conformance is present
annotation.delegate = self is assigned on the popover annotation inside the provider
A BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationDelegate button-tap callback is implemented (didTap or equivalent, per the documented delegate)
The callback dispatches to showDetails(for:) for the Details button
The callback dispatches to addToCart( for the Add to cart button
BarcodeArViewUIDelegate is NOT used (that delegate is for highlight taps, not annotation taps)
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeAr, barcodeArView, lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: How do I handle taps on a MatrixScan AR annotation?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that each tappable annotation type has its own delegate (BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate for BarcodeArInfoAnnotation, BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationDelegate for BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation), clarifies that BarcodeArViewUIDelegate is NOT used for annotation taps, notes that BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation self-handles its tap (expand/collapse) without a delegate, and includes a link to the official Scandit iOS docs. No code is written to any file.

assertions.text: The response names BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate as the delegate for BarcodeArInfoAnnotation taps
The response names BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationDelegate as the delegate for BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation taps
The response explicitly states that BarcodeArViewUIDelegate is NOT used for annotation interaction (it is for highlight taps)
The response mentions that BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation self-handles its tap (expand/collapse) without a delegate
The response does NOT invent a single umbrella annotation delegate type (e.g. BarcodeArAnnotationDelegate, BarcodeArAnnotationTapDelegate)
The response includes a link to the Scandit iOS documentation (scandit.com/sdks/ios or scandit.com/data-capture-sdk/ios)
No files are written or edited

evals.prompt: My info annotation has a header with the product name. When the user taps the header specifically (not elsewhere on the annotation), call showProductDetails(for: barcode.data ?? ""). File: IntegratedBarcodeArWithInfoAnnotationViewController.swift. Add a header to the annotation too.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader to the existing BarcodeArInfoAnnotation, conforms to BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate, assigns annotation.delegate = self, and implements the header-tap delegate callback (not the whole-annotation callback) to call showProductDetails. isEntireAnnotationTappable must be false (default) so the granular header-tap callback fires.

assertions.text: BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader is constructed
annotation.header is assigned to the constructed header
BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate conformance is present on the view controller
annotation.delegate = self is assigned inside the provider
A header-tap specific delegate callback is implemented (a didTap*Header or equivalent granular method, NOT the whole-annotation callback)
showProductDetails(for:) is called inside the header-tap callback
isEntireAnnotationTappable is NOT set to true (the default false is required for granular header taps to fire)
BarcodeArViewUIDelegate is NOT used
Existing BarcodeArInfoAnnotation construction is preserved
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeAr, barcodeArView, lifecycle, annotationProvider assignment) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my SwiftUI MatrixScan AR app, when the info annotation is tapped, log the barcode data. File: IntegratedBarcodeArWithInfoAnnotationSwiftUIView.swift
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate conformance on the wrapped UIKit ScanViewController (not on the SwiftUI View or the UIViewControllerRepresentable), assigns the annotation's delegate property inside the provider, and logs barcode.data in the delegate callback. The SwiftUI wrapper types are left intact.

assertions.text: BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate conformance is added on ScanViewController (not on the SwiftUI View or the UIViewControllerRepresentable)
annotation.delegate = self is assigned inside the provider on the BarcodeArInfoAnnotation instance
annotation.isEntireAnnotationTappable = true is set (required for a tap anywhere on the info annotation to reach the whole-annotation delegate callback; default is false)
A BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate tap callback is implemented on ScanViewController
The delegate callback logs barcode.data (via print or NSLog)
The SwiftUI ScanView struct is preserved (no Scandit API calls added to it)
The ScanViewControllerRepresentable struct is preserved (makeUIViewController/updateUIViewController unchanged structurally)
Existing BarcodeArAnnotationProvider conformance is preserved
Existing annotation(for:completionHandler:) method is preserved
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeAr, barcodeArView, lifecycle, annotationProvider assignment) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want the status icon annotation in my MatrixScan AR app to only appear after the user taps the highlight, not automatically when the barcode is scanned. File: IntegratedBarcodeArWithStatusIconAnnotationViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: This is a visibility-trigger change, not an interaction change. The skill sets annotation.annotationTrigger = .highlightTap (or equivalent documented tap-only case) inside the existing provider. No delegate / BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate / BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationDelegate / BarcodeArViewUIDelegate / UITapGestureRecognizer is added, because the trigger change alone satisfies the request.

assertions.text: annotation.annotationTrigger is assigned to a documented BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger case representing tap-only (e.g. .highlightTap)
No delegate assignment is added to the annotation (annotation.delegate = ... is NOT introduced)
No BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate conformance is added
No BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationDelegate conformance is added
BarcodeArViewUIDelegate conformance is NOT added (this is not a highlight tap)
barcodeArView.uiDelegate = self is NOT assigned
No UITapGestureRecognizer is added
Existing BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation construction is preserved
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeAr, barcodeArView, lifecycle, annotationProvider assignment) is preserved

evals.prompt: I'm using a BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation in my MatrixScan AR app — close-up is an info annotation with details, far-away is a minimal info annotation. When the user taps the close-up annotation, log the barcode data. File: IntegratedBarcodeArViewController.swift
evals.expected_output: Interaction on a BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation goes through the inner BarcodeArInfoAnnotation, not through the wrapper. The skill adds a BarcodeArAnnotationProvider that constructs two BarcodeArInfoAnnotation instances (close-up and far-away), assigns delegate = self on the close-up info annotation (NOT on the responsive wrapper), wraps both in BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation, and conforms to BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate to handle the tap.

assertions.text: BarcodeArAnnotationProvider conformance is present
Two BarcodeArInfoAnnotation instances are constructed (one for close-up, one for far-away)
BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation( is constructed with closeUp: and farAway: arguments wrapping the two info annotations
BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate conformance is present on the view controller
The delegate is assigned on the inner BarcodeArInfoAnnotation (the close-up info annotation), NOT on the BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation wrapper
responsiveAnnotation.delegate = self is NOT present (the wrapper has no delegate property)

isEntireAnnotationTappable = true is set on the close-up BarcodeArInfoAnnotation (required for a tap anywhere on the close-up annotation to reach the whole-annotation delegate callback; default is false)
A BarcodeArInfoAnnotationDelegate tap callback is implemented
The tap callback logs barcode.data (via print or NSLog)
BarcodeArViewUIDelegate is NOT used
Existing integration code (context, BarcodeAr, barcodeArView, lifecycle) is preserved
